sources.label: Emulation | Playwright
borrowed: device-class proof for tablet and phone mission-stack states with locale and timezone held constant
rejected: treating one viewport as representative of all folded-shell behavior

sources.label: Browser contexts | Playwright
borrowed: fresh contexts per proof so fold continuity is exercised explicitly
rejected: shared browser state that could hide reload or back-stack regressions

sources.label: Visual comparisons | Playwright
borrowed: deterministic tablet and mobile screenshots for the mission stack and recovery posture
rejected: unstructured screenshots without stable shell-state assertions

sources.label: Trace Viewer | Playwright
borrowed: trace-friendly interaction proofs for queue drawer, frozen overlay, and watch-window re-entry
rejected: debugging fold-state regressions without replayable browser evidence

sources.label: Understanding SC 1.4.10: Reflow | W3C
borrowed: 320px no-horizontal-overflow proof for the compact pharmacy mission stack
rejected: horizontal scrolling as an acceptable fallback for folded shell content

sources.label: Understanding SC 2.5.8: Target Size (Minimum) | W3C
borrowed: roomy touch targets and spacing for queue, support, and recovery controls
rejected: dense icon-only controls in the compact mission stack

sources.label: Understanding SC 2.4.11: Focus Not Obscured (Minimum) | W3C
borrowed: reserved layout space and hit-safe sticky dock behavior so focused or tapped controls are not obscured
rejected: letting persistent sticky chrome cover active controls

sources.label: Dialog (Modal) Pattern | APG | WAI | W3C
borrowed: bounded focus handling for the queue peek drawer and logical focus return to the invoking control

sources.label: Grid Pattern | APG | WAI | W3C
borrowed: predictable keyboard movement and structural grouping for dense folded-shell content
rejected: arbitrary tab order through dense workbench regions

sources.label: Developing a Keyboard Interface | APG | WAI | W3C
borrowed: predictable tab sequence and focus return discipline across queue, support, and dock controls
rejected: pointer-first recovery controls that lose a logical keyboard order

sources.label: Card | NHS digital service manual
borrowed: quiet bordered cards with readable heading hierarchy for folded-shell support and recovery surfaces
rejected: marketing-heavy card treatments that overpower operational content

sources.label: Notification banners | NHS digital service manual
borrowed: restrained status treatment so recovery remains explicit without becoming banner soup
rejected: stacking multiple warning banners in the folded shell

sources.label: Board layout | Linear Docs
borrowed: dense, low-noise queue scanning with fast route context rather than oversized cards
rejected: turning the folded queue into a decorative kanban miniature

sources.label: Display options | Linear Docs
borrowed: persistent view state and user-controlled density without route churn
rejected: resetting shell presentation every time the route narrows

sources.label: Nested Layouts | Vercel Academy
borrowed: persistent section chrome and nested layout continuity across child routes
rejected: duplicating shell chrome per child route or per folded state
